title: UAE Plans New Port On 'East Coast' To Avoid Iran Fire In Hormuz
content: The new port is also likely to deepen DP World's penetration in the Gulf of Oman, allowing shipments to travel in and out of the country without needing to pass through the narrow waterway between Oman and Iran.

title: "Don't Let Ketan Become Just Another Case File": A Mother's Appeal To PM Modi
content: Every day without justice deepens our pain, wrote Ketan Agarwal's mother, calling for the strictest punishment for those responsible for taking her son's life "without unnecessary delay."

title: Delhi, UP See Monsoon Pause. When Will Rain Return?
content: Experts say the primary driver behind the current dry spell is a meteorological phenomenon known as a "break monsoon" condition, coupled with a significant shift in monsoon trough.

title: "Sheikh Hasina Will Have To Go To Jail": Bangladesh Minister On Exiled Ex-PM
content: "Wherever Sheikh Hasina surrenders, whether in India or Bangladesh, she will have to go to jail first," said State Minister for Foreign Affairs Shama Obaed Islam.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 450 Points As Oil Prices Rally Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices jump to one-month high after 10% rally; Brent oil near $85 a barrel.

title: Beyond Low Cost: Why Foreigners Choose India's Healthcare Over The West
content: India's medical tourism industry is estimated to be worth $12.32 billion in 2026. It is growing much faster than the country's overall tourism sector.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Gets Warmer, Partly Cloudy Sky Expected Today
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Update: Delhi recorded a maximum temperature of 38.6 degrees Celsius on Monday

title: From A "Hello... This is Divya" WhatsApp Text To A Rs 21-Crore Crypto Fraud
content: The police have so far managed to freeze approximately Rs 2 crore, but the remaining money was allegedly moved, withdrawn, converted or used for other payments before investigators could stop it.

title: How Israel's Plan To Bring Iran Ex-President Ahmadinejad To Power Failed
content: American, Iranian, and former intelligence officials claimed that Israel's spy agency, Mossad, tried to build a relationship with Ahmadinejad after he fell out with Iran's current leadership.

title: No Political Entry: Jr NTR Clarifies Amid Buzz Over July 18 Tirupati Event
content: Jr. NTR's team has clarified that the programme is not political and focuses solely on the social initiative

title: Prashant Kishor Has Rs 96-Crore Assets, Wife Richer With Rs 102-Crore Wealth
content: Prashant Kishor, a poll strategist-turned-politician, is seeking to win the seat vacated by BJP chief Nitin Nabin a couple of months ago.

title: Woman Puts Mustard Seeds On Karnataka Judge's Chair, Arrested For Black Magic
content: Police said a case has been registered against her under the Karnataka Prevention and Eradication of Inhuman Evil Practices and Black Magic Act, 2017.

title: Anthropic Starts India Pricing For Claude AI, Pro Plan At Rs 2,000
content: The AI startup will bill Indian users in rupees instead of US dollars as it doubles down on its second-largest market after the US.

title: Centre Summons Iranian Envoy After Indian Killed In Strikes On Ship In Hormuz
content: According to the report, the tankers, Mombasa and Al Bahiyah, were struck while transiting the southern passage of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: HCLTech's New Hiring Play: 'Elite Engineers', Bigger Pay, AI-Focused Jobs
content: HCLTech's hiring announcement comes as it reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 4,624 crore for the financial quarter that ended in June.

title: 'When Will Mumma Return?' Rajasthan Maternal Deaths Tear Families Apart
content: In the last two months, 18 maternal deaths have been reported across five districts in Rajasthan, raising serious concerns about the healthcare system.

title: Audit Report Exposes Rs 3,544 Crore Overspend On Ladki Bahin Yojana
content: A recent audit by the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) found that the government spent Rs 33,237.24 crore on the Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana against an authorised budget of Rs 29,693.09 crore, resulting in an excess expenditure of Rs 3,541.16 crore.

title: India's Youngest Airline Seeks Funds As Iran War Drives Up Costs: Report
content: The US-Iran war triggered flight disruptions and a surge in jet-fuel prices - which accounts for about 40 per cent of an airline's operating costs - creating fresh financial pressure.

title: "Within 3 Minutes, It Flipped", Recalls Vietnam Boat Tragedy Survivor
content: Nirmal Kumar, one of the survivors, revealed that the boat went upside down within three minutes of boarding and barely crossing 300 meters.

title: 'Both vessels carried 30 Indian seafarers': MEA condemns attack in Hormuz that killed sailor
content: India registered a strong protest against continued attacks on vessels carrying Indian seafarers.  Two vessels, MT Al Bahiyah and MT Mombasa, were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz.  One Indian national tragically lost his life while others sustained injuries during the attacks.  The ministry summoned Iran's deputy chief of mission to lodge a strong protest.  India calls for an immediate cessation of violence and a return to dialogue.

title: SC orders separate space near MP's Bhojshala for Friday namaz between 1pm and 3pm
content: The Supreme Court directed that any ASI structural alterations require its prior permission in the Bhojshala row. Separate space for Friday prayers for Muslims adjacent to the site was also directed. Both Hindu and Muslim sides were urged to exercise restraint during proceedings.

title: Virat Kohli, Gautam Gambhir avoid interaction ahead of India vs England 1st ODI
content: Head coach Gautam Gambhir and Virat Kohli were not seen interacting during India's training. Gambhir spent considerable time with other squad members and coaching staff. Kohli engaged in a lengthy batting session and spoke with batting coach Sitanshu Kotak. Captain Shubman Gill and Rohit Sharma also had discussions with the head coach.

title: Navi Mumbai: Man killed, body chopped into pieces; wife, lover held 11 months after murder
content: Police arrested a wife and her paramour for allegedly killing her husband.  The accused murdered the man last August and dismembered his body.  They then dumped the remains in a forest in Navi Mumbai.  The crime came to light after the victim's brother grew suspicious.  Remains were recovered, and the accused are in police custody.

title: Apple lawsuit against OpenAI alleges poaching, CAD theft and stolen laptops
content: Apple has filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of corporate espionage. Former Apple employees allegedly stole trade secrets and confidential product designs. One engineer exploited a bug to access Apple's internal network storage. Another former executive coached candidates to bring proprietary designs and supplier details. OpenAI stated it has no interest in other companies' trade secrets.

title: Anupam Kher defends Ram Mandir remarks amid backlash: 'I'm not scared'
content: Actor Anupam Kher has refused to retract his controversial remarks on alleged Ram Mandir donation irregularities. He stated his words were taken out of context and insists he spoke with honesty. Kher asserted that external pressure will not alter his firm stance on the matter. He clarified that his comments were made responsibly and without fear of backlash. The actor remains committed to speaking his truth regardless of public agreement.

title: How bees helped engineers solve flaw regarded as Solar panels’ biggest shortcoming
content: Engineers developed a new solar panel design inspired by bee honeycombs. This innovative three-dimensional concave structure redirects reflected light back into the panel.  Researchers report significant power output increases compared to conventional flat panels. The flexible metamaterial allows integration into curved surfaces and vehicles. This advancement expands solar power applications beyond traditional rooftop installations.

title: 'Taunted woman, harassed': Nashik family chased for 15km after altercation with group
content: A family visiting Bhavli Dam faced harassment from local men. An argument escalated after a woman was taunted by some individuals. More locals joined the group and confronted the visiting family members. The family was then chased for many kilometers by the group. Police have since arrested some individuals in connection with this incident.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sanchez is more than a billionaire’s partner
content: Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sanchez marked their first wedding anniversary with a celebration near Fiji.  The couple relocated to Miami and purchased significant property on Indian Creek Island.  Their mornings begin with a no-phone policy and gratitude journaling.  They actively engage in philanthropy through their various funds and initiatives.  Both Bezos and Sanchez share a passion for space exploration and fashion.

title: Indian woman’s US middle-class home tour goes viral online
content: An Indian woman's US home tour sparked debate online about the definition of middle class. Her family's comfortable American home contrasted sharply with Indian housing realities. Many Indians found her definition of ordinary quite extraordinary. Affordable housing is a significant challenge for urban Indian middle-class families. This stark difference in living standards generated considerable discussion.

title: Canada govt warns banks AI models like Claude Mythos could raise cyber risks
content: Canada's banking regulator has alerted major financial institutions about advanced AI risks. Frontier AI models like Anthropic's Claude Mythos could increase cyber threats significantly. Banks are developing their own AI defenses to counter these evolving cybersecurity challenges. The government has access to Anthropic's Project Glasswing, though bank deployment remains unclear. Anthropic's recent admission about Claude's internal workspace fuels AI consciousness debates.

title: 'Meri poori duniya chali gayi': Ketan Agarwal’s mother writes to PM Modi, seeks justice
content: Ketan Agarwal's mother has written an emotional letter to Prime Minister Narendra Modi. She is seeking justice and the strictest action against those responsible for his brutal murder. The family has suffered immense pain and an irreparable void since Ketan's untimely death. Investigators are examining complicated personal circumstances and alleged double lives in the ongoing probe. The grieving family continues to await justice for their beloved son Ketan.

title: 'Not a brick in my name': Ram Temple treasurer amid donation theft row
content: Ayodhya Ram Temple Trust treasurer Swami Govind Dev Giri denied resignation reports. He stated that the theft of donations is likely around Rs 3 crore. Champat Rai resigned voluntarily after realizing his negligence in the matter. An Ayodhya court extended the judicial custody of eight accused individuals. The Supreme Court has also issued notices regarding the alleged misappropriation of funds.

title: Lights out: How Iran quietly moved millions of barrels through Hormuz under America's nose
content: According to ship-tracking data, six US-sanctioned supertankers with a carrying capacity of 12 million barrels of crude combined have crossed the Strait of Hormuz into the Gulf of Oman over the past week while keeping their transponders switched off.

title: Why US attack on Iran's submarine repair facility could change naval warfare forever
content: Unmanned systems are increasingly becoming complex and autonomous weapons. Iran is launching drones at its neighbours, bypassing expensive air defence systems. The United States used Corsair Unmanned Surface Vessels in an offensive role against Iran. Ukrainians have also extensively used unmanned systems in the Black Sea. These autonomous systems are fundamentally changing the face of modern conflict.

title: Rupee back in red! Impact of RBI’s forex boost fades; currency breaches 96 versus dollar
content: The rupee has weakened by more than 0.8% so far in the current fiscal year. On Monday, the one-month forward premium on the rupee stood at 3.17%, whereas the one-year forward premium was 2.83%.

title: France, England, Spain or Argentina? How a $5.3 billion betting market is calling World Cup
content: With four footballing heavyweights remaining, one of the biggest questions among fans is simple: Who is most likely to lift the trophy? Two of the largest prediction platforms, Polymarket and Kalshi, have attracted enormous interest throughout the tournament, with a combined betting volume of more than $5.3 billion across various FIFA World Cup markets. Around $4.2 billion has been wagered on Polymarket and $1.1 billion on Kalshi across predictions ranging from tournament winner and Golden Boot to Golden Ball, Best Young Player, Golden Glove and Fair Play awards.

title: Referee dies at 38, weeks after FIFA World Cup removal
content: Dutch football referee Rob Dieperink has died at the young age of thirty-eight. He was recently removed from the FIFA World Cup 2026 officials list. Dieperink was arrested in Britain following an investigation which was later closed. The Netherlands football association and FIFA have expressed their deep sadness. Dieperink had a notable career, officiating many professional matches internationally.

title: Can a landlord refuse to rent to a live-in couple? Here's what the law says
content: In India, unmarried couples seeking housing often face obstacles as landlords and housing societies hold on to outdated societal norms. Although the Supreme Court acknowledges live-in relationships as legal for consenting adults, the absence of nationwide anti-discrimination laws allows personal biases to persist. To safeguard themselves, couples are encouraged to establish written agreements and keep thorough records of their payments.

title: Institutional investments in real estate jump 58% to record $4.1 billion in January-June: Report
content: In the first half of 2026, India’s real estate sector saw unprecedented institutional investments totaling $4.1 billion. This remarkable growth was driven by both heightened domestic capital involvement and an influx of foreign investors. The commercial real estate sector garnered the majority of funding, particularly due to the presence of Global Capability Centres. Strong investment trends are anticipated to continue, as market uncertainties gradually diminish.

title: 'What wrong did Vaibhav Sooryavanshi do?': World Cup-winner blasts Shreyas, Gambhir
content: Former India captain Kris Srikkanth criticised the team management's handling of young player Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. He questioned the decision to drop the fifteen-year-old after just three T20 international matches. Srikkanth compared this situation to his own captaincy experience with Sachin Tendulkar. He emphasized the importance of backing young talent for confidence building. The former opener expressed strong disappointment with the current leadership's approach.

title: After 18 years in the US, ex-Meta employee returned to Bengaluru. Here's why
content: After spending 18 years building a successful technology career in the US with companies like Microsoft, LinkedIn and Meta, engineering leader Balaji Gururajan returned to Bengaluru with his family. His decision was driven not by financial goals but by a desire to be closer to ageing parents and spend more time with his children. His journey highlights how personal priorities can reshape the meaning of success beyond career achievements and global opportunities.

title: Kim Kardashian reportedly envious after Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
content: Kim Kardashian reportedly drew inspiration from Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. She is now considering her own future wedding plans with Lewis Hamilton. The lavish event sparked Kardashian's thoughts about a grand celebration. She envisions a guest list exceeding one thousand people. Neither couple has publicly commented on these reports.

title: Tom Segura and Christina P split after 18 years: Here's what we know
content: Tom Segura and Christina P have reportedly separated after 18 years of marriage, according to TMZ. The news has surprised many fans who have followed the couple’s journey through comedy, podcasts and television projects. While the pair have remained largely private about the situation, reports suggest they will continue working together, leaving plenty of questions still unanswered.

title: Chatur from '3 Idiots' urges people to support Sonam Wangchuk amid his hunger strike
content: Actor Omi Vaidya has expressed his endorsement of Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike, reminding everyone that the beloved character Phunsukh Wangdu was based on Wangchuk's ideals. As Wangchuk's health deteriorates during his protest at Jantar Mantar over examination irregularities, he calls for ministerial resignations. Activist Prashant Bhushan has further amplified Vaidya's message across social media platforms.

title: Team India look to shake off T20I hangover as road to ODI World Cup begins
content: After a disastrous T20I tour, India begin their ODI World Cup build-up against England at Edgbaston. With injuries forcing changes, Shubman Gill's side will look to reset, test combinations and lean on the experience of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and Jasprit Bumrah as preparations for the 2027 World Cup gather pace.

title: Alia Bhatt joins Sohum Shah, Nawazuddin Siddiqui for 'Tumbbad 2'
content: Sohum Shah confirmed Alia Bhatt's casting in the highly anticipated Tumbbad 2 sequel.  She expressed excitement about joining the dark fantasy universe created by the makers.  Nawazuddin Siddiqui was previously confirmed as another major addition to the cast.  The film will continue exploring the mythology surrounding the cursed deity Hastar.  Tumbbad 2 is scheduled for a theatrical release on December 3, 2027.

title: Pahalgam terror attack: NIA court issues non-bailable warrant against LeT founder Hafiz Saeed
content: A special NIA court has issued a non-bailable warrant for Hafiz Saeed. This action stems from the agency's ongoing investigation into the Pahalgam terror attack. The Pakistan-based chief of Lashkar-e-Taiba is accused of masterminding the attack. He is also wanted for the 2008 Mumbai terror attacks. The warrant seeks his arrest for a fair and complete investigation.

title: Ex-US Navy officer, Indian wife: What we know about American caught near Nepal border
content: Uttar Pradesh police registered a case against American national Jordan Brown. He was apprehended for attempting to enter Nepal without proper authorization. Brown claimed he lost his passport in Thailand and traveled to India by sea. He had been residing in Goa and Bengaluru before his apprehension. Officials are investigating his claims and lack of documentation.

title: Gold price prediction today: Will gold prices continue to fall? Check July 14 outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices will continue to be influenced by developments on the US-Iran conflict front in the near-term, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Living in relative's house for free? You can't later claim tenancy rights, Gujarat HC rules
content: A relative living rent-free in a house cannot claim tenancy rights. The Gujarat High Court upheld a written agreement clarifying the arrangement. The court rejected claims of illiteracy and threats as belated and unsupported. The occupant's possession became illegal after the agreed period expired. The court modified compensation dates but confirmed the eviction order.

title: SC restores widow's rights, says suspicious registered wills need judicial scrutiny
content: Allowing an appeal in Sardari Lal v. Bishan Dass & Ors., a Bench of Justices Manoj Misra and K.V. Viswanathan restored the concurrent findings of the Trial Court and the First Appellate Court, which had rejected a Will executed by an illiterate farmer in favour of distant relatives while completely disinheriting his widow.

title: France equips Rafale with low-cost rockets to tackle Shahed-style drone threats
content: France has successfully equipped its Rafale fighter jets with advanced 68mm laser-guided rockets. This innovative weaponry offers a cost-effective means to tackle rising drone threats. Under the Ladac program, the French Air Force is enhancing its anti-drone capabilities. This swift enhancement demonstrates strategic lessons drawn from recent conflicts, while countries like the US and UK are also incorporating similar systems into their arsenals.

title: NCP rift out in open: Sunetra Pawar’s takeover challenged, Praful Patel calls for 'corrective steps'
content: A legal notice challenges Sunetra Pawar's election as national NCP president.  Praful Patel acknowledged a leadership vacuum and the need for corrective measures.  The notice questions the validity of the election process and constitutional protocols followed.  It also highlights inconsistencies in party submissions to the Election Commission.  The party has fifteen days to declare the election and office-bearer list null and void.

title: Exclusive | 'This moment belongs to my father': Yash Thakur on maiden India call-up
content: From dedicating his maiden India call-up to his late father to reuniting with bowling coach Morne Morkel, Yash Thakur reflects on the journey behind his biggest career milestone. In an exclusive interview with TimesofIndia. com, the Punjab Kings pacer opens up about Shreyas Iyer's faith in his bowlers, the winning mindset instilled by Ricky Ponting, and why pressure situations bring out the best in him.

title: Jharkhand: 3-year-old's rape hushed up by Panchayat over feast 'fine' from suspect
content: Panchayat members in Gumla allegedly accepted Rs 20,000 to conceal a child's rape.  The money was reportedly used for a feast after the suspect's family paid the fine.  Police arrested the teenage suspect after visiting the village on Sunday evening.  A magisterial probe will verify if panchayat members shielded the suspect for cash.  Authorities are investigating all aspects of the alleged crime and the feast.

title: 'Possible target for a nice big fat shot': Trump threatens strike on key Iran N-site
content: President Trump indicated American forces might soon strike Iran's Pickaxe Mountain site. He stated the US closely monitors the heavily fortified location with advanced surveillance. Recent US strikes against Iran are described as retaliation for targeting commercial vessels. The naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz has also been reinstated by the US Navy. Iran's nuclear situation is reportedly not progressing well, according to officials.

title: 'Responsibility to protect Americans': Trump tells US Congress fighting with Iran resumed
content: US President Trump has updated Congress on the recommencement of military action against Iran, marking the beginning of a new sixty-day operational phase for US forces. This escalation follows reported attacks on commercial shipping, attributed to Iran's supposed violation of a ceasefire. In response, the US has carried out several strikes, while Democrats assess potential legal actions to enforce compliance from the administration.

title: 'Sorry': Why McCullum apologised to fans ahead of IND vs ENG 1st ODI
content: Brendon McCullum has apologised to England fans after his removal as Test coach. He accepted the decision, stating the team's results were not good enough. McCullum admitted disappointment over failing to win major series against India and Australia. He took full responsibility for the team's culture and tactical performances. The former New Zealand captain will continue as England's white-ball coach.

title: CBSE defends three-language policy in SC, says nearly half of affiliated schools meet norms
content: CBSE has defended its three-language policy before the Supreme Court, stating that 47.3% of its affiliated schools already offer two or more Indian languages in Class 9, while 99.19% have at least one Indian-language teacher. As the court hears challenges to the policy, CBSE and NCERT have maintained that implementation measures, textbooks and transition support are already in place.

title: No more Chemburs: How can Indian cities ensure their trees do not ‘turn against’ their people
content: Even as the verdict of the inquiry committee in Chembur tree fall incident comes in, a stroll through three other Indian metros, Chennai, Bengaluru and Delhi, to find out if they are proactive enough with their trees to prevent a Chembur on their soil

title: Supreme Court mulls 24X7 emergency protocol to protect life, liberty
content: Access to justice cannot wait for office hours in the background of credible reports of late-night arrests, early morning demolition drives and deportation or executive actions undertaken during weekends or holidays

title: Nagaland IED blast: Search operations intensified; NSCN-IM condemns ‘cowardly terrorist act’
content: Havildar Mohd Iqbal from Jammu and Kashmir was killed, and four other Assam Rifles personnel were injured in the IED blast close to a vehicle of the paramilitary force near Sukhovi July 13

title: CBSE sees ‘practical’ way out of three-language resource crunch through retirees, ‘suitable’ PGs
content: CBSE said 47.3% already offer two or more native Indian languages and 99.9% of schools already have at least one Indian language teacher

title: Data show Sanskrit and Hindi are choices for third language in many Indian schools
content: Data hint that Sanskrit and Hindi are preferred by default due to a lack of supply and demand, even if they are not imposed

title: Madhya Pradesh Bhojshala complex: Supreme Court suggests temporary prayer space for Muslims until case decided
content: Supreme Court directed that the Archaeological Survey of India would not make any structural alterations to the disputed structure without leave of the apex court

title: CBSE’s three-language formula triggers concerns among students, parents
content: Stakeholders fear mandatory inclusion of two Indian languages from Class IX could disrupt academic plans, limit foreign language options and increase pressure on learners

title: Traffic diversion for Miyapur-Allwyn flyover  trial run exposes bottleneck, police propose additional service lane to tackle congestion
content: Existing carriageway at Miyapur junction could not handle diverted traffic on a corridor used by nearly five lakh vehicles every day

title: Bangladeshi issue needs bilateral solution, not ‘pushback drama’ says Akhil Gogoi
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma is keeping the illegal immigration issue alive without establishing any legal mechanism, the Raijor Dal MLA says

title: Punjab Congress: Why the Channi-Raja Warring leadership battle matters | Explained
content: Responding to the controversy, Warring says there should indeed be no ‘sleeper cell’ or ‘compromised leader’ within the Congress

title: AAP chief Kejriwal urges Wangchuk to end hunger strike; will meet him on July 16
content: "Sonam Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for some days, and his health is deteriorating. He is an asset of the country, and I appeal to him to end his hunger strike. There are other ways also for the struggle," says Arvind Kejriwal

title: Kerala actor assault case: High Court rejects prime accused Sunil’s plea to suspend jail term
content: Division Bench holds that finding of Ernakulam Sessions Court is reasonable and that there are no grounds to justify suspension of petitioner’s sentence

title: The political storm in Punjab surrounding ‘Satluj’ movie | Explained
content: As the controversy surrounding the film and its removal from an OTT platform continues, political organisations, youth outfits and religious bodies have been organising public screenings of the film in villages and towns of Punjab

title: Kerala BJP councillor detained under KAAPA retakes oath inside prison in unprecedented move
content: Kerala High Court earlier permitted KAAPA detainee R. Sugathan to take oath in Viyyur jail; political row erupts over unprecedented ceremony

title: Man accused in 2020 sacrilege case beheaded in Punjab
content: Mewa Singh was murdered by an unidentified assailant at his home in Ladpur Tooran village near Mandi Gobindgarh at midnight, says the police.

title: T.N. Chief Minister Vijay inaugurates MLA office in his Perambur constituency
content: He also inaugurated the e-Sevai (e-Service) centre on the premises, held discussions with officials on the integrated development projects for Perambur, and received petitions from members of the public

title: Kejriwal writes to PM, seeks meeting over E20 petrol; demands pure petrol option at fuel stations
content: In a letter to the PM, Mr. Kejriwal said he wanted to explain the technical issues and the feedback he received from people related to the matter during his proposed meeting

title: EAM Jaishankar launches India’s UNSC campaign for 2028-29 term
content: Elections for the 2028-29 term will be held in June next year, when India and Tajikistan will compete for the sole seat in the Asia-Pacific Group

title: India supports UN membership for Palestine, says MEA
content: India’s position on Palestine was apprised by Sripriya Ranganathan, a secretary at the External Affairs Ministry, during a ministerial meeting of the Palestine Donor Group, held in Brussels July 13

title: Bankipur assembly by-election: Prashant Kishor and his wife declare total assets of over ₹208 crore
content: The poll strategist-turned-politician has eight criminal cases pending against him, with most of the cases dealing with unlawful assembly

title: ₹150-crore sewage treatment plant to stop sewage entering Tungabhadra: Bharat
content: Industries and Commerce Minister T.G. Bharat said the 35 MLD plant, to be built under AMRUT 2.0 within 18 months, would keep the city’s sewage out of the Tungabhadra river

title: Union Ministers Rajnath, Gadkari inaugurate Lucknow-Kanpur Expressway
content: Rajnath Singh said the inauguration of Lucknow-Kanpur Expressway had resolved the long-standing problem of traffic congestion between the two cities

title: Why Tamil Nadu does not need privatisation of power distribution
content: Government funding narrows the gap between average cost of supply and average revenue realisation of the TNPDCL, says the White Paper

title: Fodder scam cases: Supreme Court refuses to revoke Lalu Prasad Yadav’s bail
content: The top court said it was not inclined  to interfere with his liberty after six years and instead directed that the pending appeals be decided preferably within six months

title: Supreme Court seeks police report on attack on lawyer in Delhi
content: The court ordered the status report ought to be filed by an officer not below the rank of Deputy Commissioner of Police.

title: KPSC row in Karnataka: How did the ‘irregularities’ unfold?
content: A daughter of the now-suspended KPSC chairman is accused of falsely claiming reservation benefits under OBC Category 3B to facilitate her selection as Junior Engineer (Civil) in the Department of Industries and Commerce in March 2024.

title: Bengaluru Assembly segments lag in enumeration form distribution as Karnataka SIR reaches 82% coverage
content: Among the Assembly constituencies with lower distribution rates, Bommanahalli recorded the lowest progress at 35.01%, followed by Bangalore South (39.95%) and Govindarajanagar (40.25%)

title: Poompuhar to launch gifting, export initiatives; collaborate with Reliance for sale of crafts by T.N. artisans
content: Poompuhar is collaborating with Reliance Retail, which will procure handicraft products and showcase across its retail outlets. Poompuhar is making a strong push into gifting segment, with the initiative set to be launched ahead of Deepavali.

title: Migrant worker from Bihar dies in furnace explosion at Gummidipoondi copper recycling unit
content: Five other migrant workers — all from Bihar — sustained minor burn injuries and are undergoing treatment

title: Jaishankar discusses global developments, including West Asia, Ukraine with UN Chief Guterres
content: Mr. Jaishankar launched India’s official campaign for a non-permanent seat at the UN Security Council for 2028-29 at an event at the world body’s headquarters in the United Nations that was attended by UN ambassadors, diplomats and officials

title: ‘Boat flipped in three minutes,’ recounts Vietnam crash survivor upon arrival in Chennai
content: A speedboat carrying 32 Indian tourists and four local crew members capsized near Hon May Rut Ngoai off Phu Quoc Island, killing 15 Indian tourists

title: Maharashtra rainfall deficit continues; El Niño impact cited
content: Forecasts for the coming week indicate that only light to moderate rain may occur in isolated pockets, with no weather warnings issued for July 13 or July 14

title: Fadnavis visits Pawan Kalyan in Mumbai hospital after shoulder surgery
content: The Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister underwent arthroscopic repair of a right shoulder rotator cuff injury on July 11; the hospital expects full recovery over four months

title: Karnataka will establish India’s first government-driven AI university, says Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar
content: Mr. Shivakumar said the proposed university would play a pivotal role in building world-class AI talent, advancing research and strengthening collaboration between academia, industry and government

title: Autonomous hill councils to be extended to all seven districts in Ladakh
content: The Ladakh administration has added 17 additional tehsils taking the total to 32; Chief Secretary Ashish Kundra says several rounds of discussions have been held with representatives of religious organisations and civil society bodies over constitutional safeguards

title: SIR digitisation reaches 18% in Secunderabad Cantonment, political parties briefed on exercise
content: Secunderabad Cantonment has around 2.45 lakh to 2.48 lakh electors on its rolls. CEO said the distribution of enumeration forms has been completed in all households

title: Elderly man killed in elephant attack in Coimbatore district
content: The septuagenarian had gone to a nearby stream to attend nature’s call when a lone wild elephant attacked him

title: Who is Anil Menon, the Indian-origin NASA astronaut of Malayali descent set to travel to space?
content: Anil Menon, NASA’s first astronaut of Malayali origin, embarks on a historic ISS mission, conducting groundbreaking research in space.

title: Survey for Bidadi Township ends in chaos as women farmers chase officials with brooms
content: With this, the row over Bidadi township, championed by Chief Minister D. K. Shivakumar and has become a political flashpoint with Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy, has only escalated further.

title: Three get five years’ jail for red sanders smuggling in Tirupati
content: The Red Sanders Special Court sentenced the three, from Tirupati district, and fined them ₹3 lakh each in a 2017 case of smuggling from the Seshachalam reserve forest

title: Pune building collapse: FIR against two officials of private company for negligence, safety lapses
content: "As no security measures were adopted, a portion of SLF [mound of legacy waste] collapsed on the three-storey building on July 8, claiming nine lives," the FIR states

title: ‘When will PM Modi speak about people’s pain’: Congress slams government on inflation rise
content: Over the last 12 years of the Modi government, inflation has disrupted the budgets of ordinary families. Government data itself now reveals that retail inflation hit a 17-month high of 4.38% in June, said Jairam Ramesh

title: Supreme Court agrees to urgently hear Greater Bengaluru Authority’s plea to postpone civic polls due to SIR
content: Appearing before a Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, senior advocate Kapil Sibal, in an oral mentioning, said the elections were due on August 31, and it would be “seemingly impossible” to prepare for the polls while the massive SIR exercise was on.

title: Three youths killed as two-wheeler hits tree in East Godavari
content: The three, from Gadarada in Korukonda mandal, were on their way to Rajamahendravaram to buy food for a birthday when their two-wheeler hit a tree on Sunday night

title: Why are rival groups of Trinamool Congress laying claim over party funds | Explained
content: Trinamool Congress is one the richest political parties in the country with hundreds of crores lying in its bank accounts

title: Telangana to train 80,000 minority students in AI and digital safety free of cost
content: The programme, a collaboration between the Telangana government, Doxa Consulting Private Limited (DCPL) and MASK NextGen Inc, will be rolled out across 205 TMREIS residential schools from August 15
